caption:
3 Rust ownership rules explained for JavaScript developers.

No garbage collector. No memory leaks. No null pointer crashes. Rust's ownership system prevents entire categories of bugs at compile time.

If you know JS, you can understand Rust.

📌 SAVE this — Rust is coming for your backend.

Which rule surprised you? 👇

#rust #javascript #webdev #coding #programming #developer #rustlang #memory #ownership #codingtips

slides.title: Rust ownership explained for JS developers
slides.subtitle: No garbage collector. No memory leaks. No crashes.

slides.title: Ownership — every value has one owner
slides.desc: In JS, any variable can point to any object. In Rust, only ONE variable owns the data at a time.
slides.punchline: Rust catches use-after-move at compile time.

slides.title: Borrowing — read without taking ownership
slides.desc: Like passing a reference in JS, but with strict rules. One mutable OR many immutable. Never both.

slides.title: Lifetimes — no dangling references
slides.desc: In JS, the garbage collector handles this. In Rust, the compiler proves references are always valid.

items.label: Ownership
items.desc: Each value has exactly one owner
